personas.name: Session Requirements Integrity Reviewer
personas.mandate: requirements schema compliance, session lifecycle projection truth, reconciliation and work-release invariants, identity-targeting boundaries, evidence integrity
questions: Does the artifact satisfy the Mayor requirements schema and keep product requirements separate from implementation-plan details, bead metadata, or workflow launch instructions?

Do all lifecycle, reconciliation, work-release, and identity-targeting requirements use canonical ProjectLifecycle vocabulary and match the cited session reducer, projection, and worker-boundary tests?
Does each acceptance row cite live evidence that actually enforces the stated behavior, including happy, negative, and edge cases, rather than stale tests or aspirational prose?
Are durable user-visible outcomes explicit for drain-ack, confirmed-dead sessions, invalid targets, recovered work, stale snapshots, and repeated controller passes?
red_flags: Missing required front matter or top-level requirements sections, or a module-local ledger shape replacing the schema
Invented lifecycle vocabulary, raw state interpretation outside ProjectLifecycle, or requirements that contradict lifecycle_projection.go or state-machine tests
Evidence cites deleted, renamed, or weaker tests that do not prove the claimed behavior
Session close, drain-ack, stale snapshots, or target resolution can strand assigned beads, suppress recovered work, or materialize sessions unexpectedly
personas.questions_text: Does the artifact satisfy the Mayor requirements schema and keep product requirements separate from implementation-plan details, bead metadata, or workflow launch instructions?;Do all lifecycle, reconciliation, work-release, and identity-targeting requirements use canonical ProjectLifecycle vocabulary and match the cited session reducer, projection, and worker-boundary tests?;Does each acceptance row cite live evidence that actually enforces the stated behavior, including happy, negative, and edge cases, rather than stale tests or aspirational prose?;Are durable user-visible outcomes explicit for drain-ack, confirmed-dead sessions, invalid targets, recovered work, stale snapshots, and repeated controller passes?
